query: What is the maximum capacity threshold for a  generator in MISO to be eligible for the fast-track interconnection process in MISO, according to MISO Tariff Attachment X Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP)? Cite the relevant source report and section for your answer
final_answer:
The answer is clearly and directly stated in the document retrieved. Here is the full citation and answer:

---

## Maximum Capacity Threshold for Fast-Track Interconnection in MISO: **5 MW (five megawatts)**

### Source
**MISO FERC Electric Tariff, Attachment X – Generator Interconnection Procedures (GIP)**
Effective: **January 31, 2025** (Version 167.0.0)
URL: [https://cdn.misoenergy.org/Attachment%20X%20-%20GIP%20(Effective%20January%2031%202025)330116.pdf](https://cdn.misoenergy.org/Attachment%20X%20-%20GIP%20(Effective%20January%2031%202025)330116.pdf)

### Relevant Section: **Section 1 – Definitions**

The GIP provides two interrelated definitions that establish the 5 MW threshold:

> **"Fast Track Process"** shall mean the procedure for evaluating an Interconnection Request for a **certified Small Generating Facility no larger than five MW** that includes the screen set forth in Section 14, customer options meeting, and optional supplemental review.

> **"Small Generating Facility"** shall mean a Generating Facility that has an **aggregate net Generating Facility Capacity of no more than five MW** and meets the requirements of Section 14 and Appendix 3.

### Key Points

| Feature | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Maximum capacity threshold** | **≤ 5 MW** (aggregate net capacity) |
| **Applicable process** | Fast Track Process (Section 14 of the GIP) |
| **Facility type** | Certified Small Generating Facility |
| **Sub-process for very small inverter-based units** | ≤ 10 kW inverter-based facilities use the even more streamlined **10 kW Inverter Process** (Appendix 4) |

### Summary

According to **Section 1 (Definitions)** of MISO's Attachment X GIP (effective January 31, 2025), a generator must have an aggregate net capacity of **no more than 5 MW** to be eligible for the Fast Track interconnection process. Generators above this threshold must go through MISO's standard Definitive Planning Phase (DPP) process. A separate, even more streamlined pathway (the 10 kW Inverter Process) applies to certified inverter-based facilities **no larger than 10 kW**.
